message.content:
can you research metabases custom columns and tell me why this is giving the following error:

case([Predicted Nrr]<.8, "High Risk", [Predicted Nrr]>=.8 AND [Predicted Nrr]<.95,"Medium Risk", [Predicted Nrr]>=.95 AND < 1, "Low Risk", "Expected Expansion")

Expected expression

content.text: I'll research Metabase custom columns and analyze your CASE expression to identify the syntax error.

input.query: Metabase custom columns CASE expression syntax documentation

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Metabase custom columns CASE expression syntax documentation"

I'll search for documentation on Metabase custom columns CASE expression syntax.

Links: [{"title":"Custom expressions | Metabase Documentation","url":"https://www.metabase.com/docs/latest/questions/query-builder/expressions"},{"title":"Case | Metabase Documentation","url":"https://www.metabase.com/docs/latest/questions/query-builder/expressions/case"},{"title":"Add custom columns | Metabase Learn","url":"https://www.metabase.com/learn/metabase-basics/getting-started/custom-columns"},{"title":"Metabase Custom Column Case Guide — Restack","url":"https://www.restack.io/docs/metabase-knowledge-metabase-custom-column-case"},{"title":"Create custom column based on condition - Metabase Discussion","url":"https://discourse.metabase.com/t/create-custom-column-based-on-condition/15915"},{"title":"List of expressions | Metabase Documentation","url":"https://www.metabase.com/docs/latest/questions/query-builder/expressions-list"},{"title":"Custom Column Case Expression Doesn't Save · Issue #44584 · metabase/metabase","url":"https://github.com/metabase/metabase/issues/44584"},{"title":"Metabase Custom Expression Guide — Restack","url":"https://www.restack.io/docs/metabase-knowledge-metabase-custom-expression-case"},{"title":"Tutorial: Custom expressions | Metabase Learn","url":"https://www.metabase.com/learn/metabase-basics/querying-and-dashboards/questions/custom-expressions"},{"title":"What is a custom expression?","url":"https://www.metabase.com/glossary/custom-expression"}]

Based on the search results, I can provide you with comprehensive documentation about Metabase custom columns CASE expression syntax:

## Metabase CASE Expression Syntax

The CASE expression in Metabase is used for conditional logic in custom columns, following the pattern `case(condition, result, [additional conditions and outputs]..., [default_output])`.

### Basic Syntax

You can use conditionals with the case function (alias if): `case([Size] = "L", "LARGE", [SIZE] = "M", "MEDIUM", "SMALL")`

The basic structure is:
- First argument: condition to test
- Second argument: value to return if condition is true
- Additional pairs of conditions and values can follow
- Last argument (optional): default value if no conditions match

### Common Use Cases

#### 1. Simple Categorization
For example: `case([Status] = 'Active', 'Current', 'Inactive', 'Past')` - This will label each row as 'Current' if the status is 'Active', and 'Past' otherwise.

#### 2. Bucketing Numeric Ranges
Example for creating buckets: `case([Amount] >= 0 AND [Amount] <= 9, "0-9", [Amount] >= 10 AND [Amount] <= 19, "10-19", [Amount] >= 20 AND [Amount] <= 29, "20-29", [Amount] >= 30 AND [Amount] <= 39, "30-39", [Amount] >= 40 AND [Amount] <= 49, "40-49", "50+")`

#### 3. Complex Multi-Field Conditions
For multiple field conditions: `case([Has Wings] = TRUE AND [Has Face] = TRUE, 'Bird', [Has Wings] = TRUE AND [Has Face] = FALSE, 'Plane', [Has Wings] = FALSE AND [Has Face] = TRUE, 'Superman', 'Unknown')`

#### 4. Creating New/Existing Categories
Example: `case([Weeks since creation] < 52, "New", "Existing")` - This creates a column that has "New" for events from accounts that are less than a year (52 weeks) old, and "Existing" otherwise

### Important Notes

All outputs in a CASE expression must have the same data type. Avoid mixing strings, booleans, and numbers in the outputs.

Column names should be included inside square brackets, like this: [Name of Column].

You can combine case with aggregate functions to only aggregate rows that meet your conditions, such as: `distinct(case([Status] = "Shipped", [Order ID]))`.

### How to Use in Metabase

To use the custom expression editor, create a Custom Column (where the custom expression is used as a Field Formula to calculate values for the new column), or click on Filter or Summarize and select Custom Expression.

The CASE expression is a powerful tool for creating conditional logic in your Metabase queries without needing to write SQL directly, making it accessible for users to create sophisticated data transformations and categorizations.

Based on the search results, I can provide you with comprehensive documentation about Metabase custom columns CASE expression syntax:

## Metabase CASE Expression Syntax

The CASE expression in Metabase is used for conditional logic in custom columns, following the pattern `case(condition, result, [additional conditions and outputs]..., [default_output])`.

### Basic Syntax

You can use conditionals with the case function (alias if): `case([Size] = "L", "LARGE", [SIZE] = "M", "MEDIUM", "SMALL")`

The basic structure is:
- First argument: condition to test
- Second argument: value to return if condition is true
- Additional pairs of conditions and values can follow
- Last argument (optional): default value if no conditions match

### Common Use Cases

#### 1. Simple Categorization
For example: `case([Status] = 'Active', 'Current', 'Inactive', 'Past')` - This will label each row as 'Current' if the status is 'Active', and 'Past' otherwise.

#### 2. Bucketing Numeric Ranges
Example for creating buckets: `case([Amount] >= 0 AND [Amount] <= 9, "0-9", [Amount] >= 10 AND [Amount] <= 19, "10-19", [Amount] >= 20 AND [Amount] <= 29, "20-29", [Amount] >= 30 AND [Amount] <= 39, "30-39", [Amount] >= 40 AND [Amount] <= 49, "40-49", "50+")`

#### 3. Complex Multi-Field Conditions
For multiple field conditions: `case([Has Wings] = TRUE AND [Has Face] = TRUE, 'Bird', [Has Wings] = TRUE AND [Has Face] = FALSE, 'Plane', [Has Wings] = FALSE AND [Has Face] = TRUE, 'Superman', 'Unknown')`

#### 4. Creating New/Existing Categories
Example: `case([Weeks since creation] < 52, "New", "Existing")` - This creates a column that has "New" for events from accounts that are less than a year (52 weeks) old, and "Existing" otherwise

### Important Notes

All outputs in a CASE expression must have the same data type. Avoid mixing strings, booleans, and numbers in the outputs.

Column names should be included inside square brackets, like this: [Name of Column].

You can combine case with aggregate functions to only aggregate rows that meet your conditions, such as: `distinct(case([Status] = "Shipped", [Order ID]))`.

### How to Use in Metabase

To use the custom expression editor, create a Custom Column (where the custom expression is used as a Field Formula to calculate values for the new column), or click on Filter or Summarize and select Custom Expression.

The CASE expression is a powerful tool for creating conditional logic in your Metabase queries without needing to write SQL directly, making it accessible for users to create sophisticated data transformations and categorizations.

input.prompt: What is the exact syntax for CASE expressions in Metabase? Focus on how to write conditions with AND operators and multiple conditions.

content.text:
The error in your CASE expression is in the third condition: `[Predicted Nrr]>=.95 AND < 1`

The issue is that you're missing the field reference after the second AND operator. It should be:

```
case([Predicted Nrr]<.8, "High Risk", [Predicted Nrr]>=.8 AND [Predicted Nrr]<.95,"Medium Risk", [Predicted Nrr]>=.95 AND [Predicted Nrr]< 1, "Low Risk", "Expected Expansion")
```

The corrected third condition should be: `[Predicted Nrr]>=.95 AND [Predicted Nrr]< 1` instead of `[Predicted Nrr]>=.95 AND < 1`

In Metabase CASE expressions, each comparison operator needs a complete expression on both sides. You can't omit the field reference after AND.

message.content: can you print the whole thing for me with good syntax?

content.text:
```
case([Predicted Nrr] < 0.8, "High Risk", [Predicted Nrr] >= 0.8 AND [Predicted Nrr] < 0.95, "Medium Risk", [Predicted Nrr] >= 0.95 AND [Predicted Nrr] < 1, "Low Risk", "Expected Expansion")
```
